request_debug.prompt_preview:
You rerank dead-startup candidates for a slopmortem pipeline. Given a fresh pitch and K candidates retrieved by hybrid search, score each candidate from four perspectives, then return ONLY the top 5 ranked from most to least relevant.

Perspectives (each scored 0-10 with a one-sentence rationale):
- business_model: how similar are the revenue, pricing, and customer-relationship shapes?
- market: how similar are the target buyers, market size, and demand drivers?
- gtm: how similar are the go-to-

response.text: {"ranked":[{"candidate_id":"vine::social_communication","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":3,"rationale":"Vine was an ad-supported free platform for mass video sharing, not a paid 1:1 B2C marketplace with a transaction fee."},"market":{"score":5,"rationale":"Vine targeted consumer creators and fans of short-form video content, overlapping with the influencer-fan audience but not specifically beauty in APAC."},"gtm":{"score":4,"rationale":"Both relied on mobile-first consumer adoption and creator-driven growth, but Vine had no paid transaction layer or booking flow."},"stage_scale":{"score":4,"rationale":"Vine reached 200M users before shutdown, a far larger scale than a niche beauty consult marketplace would likely achieve."}},"rationale":"Vine is the closest match by sector (social communication, video, influencer-fan dynamic) but the business model is fundamentally different—free ad-supported mass platform vs. paid 1:1 video consult marketplace with a 20% take rate."},{"candidate_id":"path::social_communication","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"Path was a free social photo-sharing and messaging app with no transaction fee or marketplace monetization."},"market":{"score":4,"rationale":"Path had significant traction in Asia (Indonesia) among consumers, loosely overlapping with the APAC social/communication user base."},"gtm":{"score":3,"rationale":"Both are mobile-first B2C social apps targeting consumers, but Path had no paid commerce or influencer-monetization GTM motion."},"stage_scale":{"score":4,"rationale":"Path raised ~$65M and had ~4M users at acquisition—a modest scale somewhat comparable to an early-stage niche marketplace."}},"rationale":"Path shares the social communication sector and some APAC consumer overlap but diverges sharply on business model, monetization, and the influencer-commerce use case."},{"candidate_id":"friendster::social_communication","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"Friendster was a free social network with no transaction fee or paid consultation marketplace model."},"market":{"score":4,"rationale":"Friendster became dominant in Southeast/East Asia among consumers, offering some geographic and demographic overlap with the APAC beauty audience."},"gtm":{"score":3,"rationale":"Both relied on organic social growth in Asian markets, but Friendster had no paid commerce or creator-monetization motion."},"stage_scale":{"score":3,"rationale":"Friendster reached 115M registered users—a much larger scale than a niche paid influencer marketplace would likely attain."}},"rationale":"Friendster's APAC consumer social footprint provides a loose parallel, but the business model, monetization, and product type are entirely different from a paid beauty consult marketplace."},{"candidate_id":"yo::social_communication","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":1,"rationale":"Yo was a novelty free messaging app with no marketplace, no transaction fee, and no creator-monetization mechanism."},"market":{"score":2,"rationale":"Yo targeted a global mass consumer audience around a novelty communication gimmick, with no beauty or influencer focus."},"gtm":{"score":2,"rationale":"Yo grew via viral/Product Hunt buzz among tech enthusiasts, a very different GTM from influencer-driven beauty marketplace recruitment."},"stage_scale":{"score":4,"rationale":"Yo was a small seed-stage startup (~$2.5M raised) that shut down quickly, loosely comparable in scale to an early-stage niche marketplace."}},"rationale":"Yo shares only the social communication sector and small-scale early shutdown; business model, market, and GTM are all highly dissimilar to a paid beauty influencer consult marketplace."},{"candidate_id":"quibi::media_content","perspective_scores":{"business_model":{"score":2,"rationale":"Quibi was a subscription-based short-form streaming service, not a B2C marketplace with a per-transaction fee between creators and fans."},"market":{"score":3,"rationale":"Quibi targeted mobile-first consumers for short-form video content, loosely overlapping with the mobile video consumption behavior of the target audience."},"gtm":{"score":2,"rationale":"Quibi used studio content partnerships and broad consumer marketing, very different from influencer-driven marketplace recruitment in APAC."},"stage_scale":{"score":2,"rationale":"Quibi raised $1.75B and was a high-profile launch—far larger in capital and ambition than a niche APAC beauty consult marketplace."}},"rationale":"Quibi shares the short-form mobile video consumption angle but differs fundamentally in business model (subscription vs. transaction fee), market (global premium content vs. APAC beauty influencers), and scale."}]}
